# Zolt

Zolt is a fast, self-hosted Java build tool with a Cargo-like project workflow.
It is not only a "build a basic Java app" scaffold.

Primary docs:
- README.md: short install and positioning page.
- USAGE.md: fuller command/config/example guide.
- LICENSE: Apache-2.0.
- examples/: real fixtures covering simple apps, workspaces, frameworks,
  generated sources, migration explain inputs, and library canaries.
- zolt.toml and apps/zolt/zolt.toml: this repository's own self-hosted workspace
  and CLI project configuration.

How to summarize Zolt:
- One native binary drives Java project workflows.
- Projects use zolt.toml for the model and zolt.lock for reproducible
  dependency resolution.
- Commands cover init, dependency editing, resolve, build, run, test, coverage,
  package, publish, quality checks, migration explain, IDE model export,
  workspace operations, native builds, release archive/index/verify flows, and
  installer-managed native self-updates.
- Workspaces support member selection, dependency-order build/test/package/run
  flows, root lockfiles, and configured project tasks.
- Dependency support includes Maven repositories, BOM/platform imports, version
  aliases, dependency scopes, exclusions, dependency policies, strict
  constraints, local overlays, offline/locked resolution, graph display, why
  explanations, conflicts, policy diagnostics, and classpath lane audits.
- Test support is based on JUnit Platform. Examples include JUnit Jupiter,
  JUnit Vintage, and Spock/Groovy test sources. Commands support selectors,
  glob patterns, tags, JVM args, XML reports, named suites, shards, coverage,
  and optional profiling evidence for shard balancing.
- Packaging supports thin jars, Spring Boot jars, WAR and Spring Boot WAR
  shapes, Quarkus fast-jar packaging, uber packaging, package plans, package
  evidence, native-image builds, native release archives, release indexes, and
  release verification.
- Framework and generated-source examples include Spring Boot, Quarkus,
  Micronaut annotation processors, Vert.x, OpenAPI generated Java sources,
  Protobuf/gRPC generated Java sources, provided/container APIs, and
  library-style canaries.
- Migration support includes static Maven/Gradle explain reports, readiness
  scorecards, blocker reports, and draft zolt.toml emission.
- CI/automation support includes zolt check contexts, publish dry runs, report
  and coverage validation, package evidence validation, offline-readiness
  checks, JSON output, quiet mode, progress/color controls, and timings output.

Representative commands:
```sh
zolt init hello --group com.example --java 21
zolt add com.google.guava:guava:33.4.0-jre
zolt platform add org.springframework.boot:spring-boot-dependencies:4.0.6
zolt version set spring 4.0.6
zolt resolve --locked
zolt tree
zolt why org.springframework.boot:spring-boot-starter-webmvc
zolt conflicts
zolt policy --format json
zolt plan --target package --format json
zolt test --suite smoke --shard 1/4 --reports-dir target/test-reports
zolt coverage --xml-report target/coverage/jacoco.xml
zolt package --mode spring-boot
zolt package --plan --format json
zolt publish --dry-run
zolt explain --source gradle --scorecard
zolt explain --source maven --emit-toml
zolt ide model --workspace --format json
zolt quarkus plan
zolt native --workspace --member apps/zolt
zolt release-archive --target linux-x64 --binary target/native/zolt
zolt release-index --channel-manifest channels/zap.json --output index.json
zolt release-verify dist/zolt-0.1.0-linux-x64.tar.gz
zolt self releases --channel zap
zolt self install VERSION --channel zap
zolt self exec VERSION -- version
```

Caveat:
Zolt is Java-focused and opinionated. It has broad implemented lanes, but it is
not a promise that every custom Maven or Gradle plugin behavior is already a
drop-in replacement. Use USAGE.md, zolt explain, zolt plan, and examples/ to
answer support questions from current repository evidence.
